text:
Once upon a time, there was a sleepy old owl named Olive. Every night, she slept on a branch just outside the village of Whispering Owlets. She loved watching the world go by—chickens squawking, rabbits hopping, and the distant sound of owls.

One night, Olive heard something strange. It wasn't just the usual quiet. There was music! But where was it coming from? She listened carefully. It was her favorite song, "Moonlit Serenade." But she'd never heard it on her branch. She flew down and listened. Sure enough, a group of young owlets were serenading a lost bat who just happened to have wings like a owl. They sang about starlit nights and moonlit forests, their voices blending into an enchanting melody.

Olive's heart swelled with joy. She hadn't felt this happy in ages. She swooped down and chirped her approval. The young owlets were thrilled!
